images.alt:
Results have already started. This is from an
american woman today: My husband works for a
small manufacturing company and here in
southwestern PA that means most employees are
Trump voters. When the president of the company
sat them down today to tell them their annual
Christmas bonus would not come this year because
they now need to purchase at least a years worth of
products prior to January 21 st due to the proposed
tariffs, they did not understand. My husband said that
their president had to explain what a tariff is and how
it will directly hurt their company. They all thought the
foreign company paid the tariff. This is the level of
ignorance voting against their own interests here in
PA, where we failed American women and children
last night

images.alt: Promotional image for the HBO mini-series The Pacific

record.text:
108. The Pacific

I hadn't watched this since it first aired, as I'd recalled it was incredibly brutal in its depictions of the war in the Pacific. But also that its story was a lot more personal and piecemeal then the one told in Band of Brothers.

Both aspects remain true on the overdue rewatch.

images.alt: Promotional image for the HBO mini-series The Pacific
Film poster for the film Repo Man from 1984.

value.text:
107. Repo Man

What an odd odd movie. A very grungy 80s movie with a bunch of disparate threads all converging together in a wacky climax.
Also, am I the only one who never really buys Emilio Estevez in these kind of roles? This, and Freejack. He never really gels with the character he's playing.

record.text: My husband's little sister was killed in April. She was a new RN. She was happy. She was trans. She was not killed (directly) because she was trans. It's convoluted. But, they were thick as thieves. It's been so bad. Detectives and lawyers and banks and paperwork all while trying to grieve.

I think most, okay all, of the posts I'm seeing on the party membership of candidates questioning the validity of a vote count are missing the point: It is perfectly fine to do this. 
If you continue to do it, for years, after being asked for evidence that it happened and not producing it, well...
Then you had @shelaghfogarty.bsky.social shout "GET RID OF IT!" which became the title of a blog for quite a few years after. I then rushed to work in London Bridge straight after. Mad times.

images.alt: Mitra a kemonomimi rabbit girl, smiling happily. She has black and purple long wavy hair. In her hair is her signature red ribbon. She is wearing a long asymmetrical white dress. While holding a cupcake that has bunny ears. Behind her are hydrangea bushes with purple and blue flowers.

Adria my ffxiv viera oc who has long dark purple hair, bright blue eyes, and bunny ears. She is wearing a black shirt and baggy black pants. Leaning back as Sen a male viera with black hair and green highlights shares a pocky with her. Playing the pocky game together. He is leaning in with his hand on her knee. He is wearing a black tank top and black pants.

Adria my ffxiv viera oc who has long dark purple hair, bright blue eyes, and bunny ears. Dressed as a demon for Halloween. Laying on a red blanket while glaring with blood on her cheek. There is a spotlight on her for dramatic lighting. She is wearing a black body suit with thigh highs and a fur coat around her arms.

Azazel a vampire with blonde hair, blue eyes, and muscular build. He has a stern/displeased expression. He is holding a lit cigarette and is wearing a black trench coat and wearing a tight black turtle neck. His pants are black and so are his boots but with white soles. And he has black glove on as well.

A bearded man in mirrored aviator sunglasses and a dark brown cowboy hat poses in a yellow leather jacket and matching pants. The jacket is embellished with white leather diamonds in a traditional Mexican motif and has hair-on cow hide details on the collar and front pockets. There are also cow hide panels on the front legs of the pants.
Same man and outfit walking toward viewer. This shot shows off the full legs of the pants and the fringe on the outside leg.
Same man and outfit, standing tall and smiling.

My parents' cute new 7 month old dog Diesel. He's still getting used to his new life and was a bit growly/yappy to me but was pretty quiet whenever I stroked him. He's not up for sitting on me yet, that's my dad he's with there while I took photos. We'll be good friends in soon I hope. 🙂
#dogs #pets

images.alt: “So, supposing we hit the body with a tremendous — whether it’s ultraviolet or just very powerful light — and I think you said that that hasn’t been checked, but you’re going to test it. And then I said, supposing you brought the light inside the body, which you can do either through the skin or in some other way, and I think you said you’re going to test that, too. It sounds interesting. And then I see the disinfectant, where it knocks it out in a minute. One minute. And is there a way we can do something like that, by injection inside or almost a cleaning. Because you see it gets in the lungs, and it does a tremendous number on the lungs. So it would be interesting to check that

I'm not an academic, so we'll tweak the question: which writer are you? 

I'm B minor, aspiring toward a key change to G major.

images.alt:
Key and mode descriptions from Marc-Antoine Charpentier's Regles de Composition ca. 1682

Key with Descriptive comments

C major: gay and warlike

C minor: obscure and sad

D major: joyous and very warlike

D minor: serious and pious

Eb major: cruel and hard

E major: quarrelsome and boisterous

E minor: effeminate, amorous, plaintive

F major: furious and quick-tempered subjects

F minor: obscure and plaintive

G major: serious and magnificent

G minor: serious and magnificent

A major: joyful and pastoral

A minor: tender and plaintive

B major: harsh and plaintive

B minor: solitary and melancholic

Bb major: magnificent and joyful

Bb minor: obscure and terrible
